rulings.Case Title: GUM D. KOO ET AL VS. KEVIN W. CHENG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For: (1) An Interlocutory Judgment Ordering A Partition By Sale And The Appointment Of A Referee; Or, In The Alternative; (2) An Order To Bifurcate And Sever Plaintiffs? And Cross-Complainant Kevin Cheng?S Partition By Sale Causes Of Action
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 13, 2023 line 1 DEFENDANT KEVIN CHENG Motion For: (1) An Interlocutory Judgment Ordering A Partition By Sale And The Appointment Of A Referee; Or, In The Alternative; (2) An Order To Bifurcate And Sever Plaintiffs? And Cross-Complainant Kevin Cheng?S Partition By Sale Causes Of Action Motion for Interlocutory Judgment Ordering a Partition by Sale and the Appoint of a Referee is GRANTED. Partition by sale is the only legally available option. Request for alternative relief is moot.=(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CYRUS ESTEBAN ET AL VS. CIVIC FINANCIAL SERVICES, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 13, 2023 line 3. DEFENDANT CIVIC FINANCIAL SERVICES, LLC DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED, without leave to amend to causes of action for fraud and UCL. The fraud cause of action failed to allege scienter and intent. rulings.Case Title: 1633-1649 CABRILLO LLC ET AL VS. DAVID GRANT ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 13, 2023 line 6. DEFENDANT DAVID GRANT MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Hearing transferred to Department 503 at 10:00 a.m., on April 13, 2023. Defendants' Motion for Summary Judgment Defendants' Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Moving party failed to shift its burden and show that they are entitled to a judgment as a matter of law. No authority is cited for the proposition that Plaintiff must under the SFRO is required to do more than "pay" the relocation. No evidence is presented to establish that Plaintiff failed to "pay." Evidence presented by the Defendant at most shows that Defendant failed to receive the payment, not that Plaintiff failed to pay. Furthermore, moving party failed to cite any authority for the proposition that failure to comply with Gov. Code, 27361.6, assuming that such failure is established, renders the Constraints invalid. Furthermore, no evidence is presented to show that the operative Memorandum of Notice Regarding Withdrawal of Rental Units and the Notice of Constraints "in any way modifies, releases, or cancels the provisions of a previously recorded document." The evidence only indicates that several documents have been recorded.=(503/SKF) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: 1633-1649 CABRILLO LLC ET AL VS. KEIKO UENO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 13, 2023 line 7. DEFENDANT KEIKO UENO, MATSUNORI UENO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Hearing transferred to Department 503 at 10:00 a.m., on April 13, 2023. Defendants' Motion for Summary Judgment Defendants' Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Moving party failed to shift its burden and show that they are entitled to a judgment as a matter of law. No authority is cited for the proposition that Plaintiff must under the SFRO is required to do more than "pay" the relocation. No evidence is presented to establish that Plaintiff failed to "pay." Evidence presented by the Defendant at most shows that Defendant failed to receive the payment, not that Plaintiff failed to pay. Furthermore, moving party failed to cite any authority for the proposition that failure to comply with Gov. Code, 27361.6, assuming that such failure is established, renders the Constraints invalid. Furthermore, no evidence is presented to show that the operative Memorandum of Notice Regarding Withdrawal of Rental Units and the Notice of Constraints "in any way modifies, releases, or cancels the provisions of a previously recorded document." The evidence only indicates that several documents have been recorded.=(503/SKF) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
